burial.plot_notes: Chattanooga National Cemetery Tennessee per FA

military_service.unit: Company H, 12th Regiment, Indiana Volunteer Infantry
military_service.notes: Enlisted Aug 17, 1862. Mustered into Company H, 12th Indiana Volunteer Infantry, Aug 17, 1862. Died April 22, 1864 at Scottsboro, Alabama. Buried Chattanooga National Cemetery. Same company (Company H, 12th Indiana) as Sgt. Major John 'Jack' Hooker, who later married his widow Louisa Vanzant.

narrative_blocks.header: Died in the same company whose sergeant major
narrative_blocks.body: PVT John Brooks enlisted August 17, 1862 in Company H, 12th Regiment, Indiana Volunteer Infantry — the same company in which John 'Jack' Hooker served as Sergeant Major — and died April 22, 1864 at Scottsboro, Alabama, at approximately twenty-five years old, buried at Chattanooga National Cemetery. After the war ended, his widow Louisa Vanzant Brooks married the man who had commanded his company as its senior non-commissioned officer — Jack Hooker — a connection that made the Civil War's death and survival into an intimate family story.
